page: Google Cloud Platform c4a-highmem-16-lssd is a memory-optimized server instance featuring 16 dedicated vCPUs built on the x86_64 CPU architecture. It is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. The instance does not include integrated graphics processing units, with a GPU count of zero, and local storage is specified as none. Additionally, it does not provide complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

From a resource density perspective, the dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent processing capabilities without resource contention. The 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio positions this instance for memory-intensive workloads that require substantial memory capacity relative to compute cores. This configuration offers qualitative cost efficiency for workloads where memory capacity is the primary constraint, avoiding the need to provision excess CPU cores solely to obtain sufficient RAM.

This server configuration is suitable for memory-intensive applications such as medium-sized databases, in-memory caching layers, data buffering, and enterprise applications requiring a high memory footprint. The lack of local storage and GPU acceleration makes it less suitable for high-speed local disk caching, heavy storage-bound databases, or machine learning training workloads.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4a-highmem-16-lssd is an x86_64 memory-optimized server instance configured with 16 dedicated vCPUs and 128.0 GB of RAM. This configuration establishes a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU, designed to support memory-intensive applications without the overhead of excess compute cores. The instance features dedicated CPU allocation for predictable performance, though it lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, with both fields specified as none or zero. It also includes zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses. This resource profile is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads constrained by memory capacity rather than raw processing power or disk I/O. It is suited for in-memory databases, caching systems, and enterprise applications requiring substantial memory capacity.

tagline: An x86_64 memory-optimized virtual server featuring a high memory-to-core ratio of eight gigabytes per dedicated vCPU for memory-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 128.0 GB of system memory (8.0 GB per vCPU)
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses included
Suited for in-memory databases and caching layers
